title: The Steward Called to Account
subtitle: The wretched soul summoned to render the reckoning of its stewardship

stillness: These three words of the severe Judge — let us hear them now.
If they sounded continually in our ears, we would in no way sin.
Sit quiet, before the judgment, and make Him favorable.
gospel: A certain man was rich, who had a steward.
And the same was accused unto him,
that he had wasted his goods.
And he called him, and said to him:
What is this I hear of thee?
Render an account of thy stewardship,
for now thou canst be steward no longer.
And the steward said within himself:
What shall I do, because my lord taketh away from me the stewardship?
To dig I am not able; to beg I am ashamed.
I know what I will do.
behold: Behold: every man is the steward of God,
to whom a threefold farm is entrusted to be governed.
The first farm is this world, whose fruits are temporal goods;
guard them, lest the wicked seize them.
The second farm is your own body,
kept lest death enter to the soul through the gates of the senses.
The third farm is the soul,
to be guarded from depraved thoughts;
all her virtues are to be spent on the knowing and the love of God.
And you — you are accused before Him,
for nothing lies hidden from Him,
as though you had squandered His goods, using them ill:
spending temporal things on what is unlawful and excessive,
giving your body to its pleasures,
filling your soul with unclean thoughts.
consider: Three words Christ speaks here to the sinner — words of great anguish and dread.
The first is a word of rigid rebuke: What is this I hear of thee?
Of thee, so enriched with the gifts of grace, so forestalled with blessings,
a squanderer of my goods committed to thee?
What is this I hear — a cry resounding from the earth,
such horrible crimes, such ancient vices;
thou who hast wasted such goods, defiled thy body,
and grown old in the sins of thy soul.
The second is a word of anxious fear: Render an account of thy stewardship.
O strict word! O anxious word! O hard word of the searching trial then to be made,
when the reckoning shall be demanded of us
for the hidden thoughts of the heart, for idle words, for our deeds!
Then the heavenly books shall be opened,
then the book of conscience shall lie open,
in which memory will read whatever a man has thought, said, and done.
The third is a word of bitter sorrow: Now thou canst be steward no longer.
I think he would give the whole world
to be granted the space of a single hour, in which to repent and do some good;
but it is not given,
for that time is the time of rendering the account, not of plying the stewardship.
To dig, by penance, I am not able;
to beg, by prayer, I am ashamed.
Hear, then: This present life has the doing of the commandments; the life to come, the reward.
If you have done nothing here, in vain do you hope for the future.
Yet — I know what I will do.
Note that the heavenly mansions belong to the poor, for to them they are given;
for of the poor in spirit is the kingdom of heaven.
The rich have their houses on earth, the poor in heaven;
let the rich gladly receive the poor into their houses on earth,
that they may be received by the poor.
prayer: make my soul a farm of virtues,
lest your steward, my reason, squander the goods you have entrusted to me;
for after death I know not what meritorious thing I might do.
Forgive me the hundred barrels of oil I owe you through contemplation,
and the hundred measures of wheat I owe you through good works;
and may your poor, who love you and are conformed to you,
become my intercessors, advocates, and patrons before you,
so that when I fail in my own merits, and depart from this life,
I may be received by their merits and prayers into their houses —
into the eternal and beloved dwellings. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: O verbum strictum! O verbum anxium! O verbum durum discussionis districtæ tunc faciendæ, quando exigetur a nobis ratio de occultis cordium cogitationibus, de otiosis sermonibus, et de nostris operibus!
latinJewel.english: O strict word! O anxious word! O hard word of the searching trial then to be made, when the reckoning shall be demanded of us for the hidden thoughts of the heart, for idle words, and for our deeds!
